issue_title: NumPy boolean multiplication (to mimic an "if")
issue_body:
> This issue follows a discussion with @laem

In some formulas (@laem do you remember which one?) an array is multiplied by a array of booleans.

I'm pretty sure that numpy does some optimisations, in particular when all the values are `False`:

```python
np.array([1,2,4]) * np.array([False, False, False]) => array([0, 0, 0])
```

But when the code of the formula is like:

```python
def function(self, simulation, period):
   numbers = simulation.calculate('numbers', period)
   booleans = simulation.calculate('booleans', period)
   return period, numbers * booleans
```

Even if `booleans` is an array of booleans all `False`, `numbers` will have been calculated.
Of course there are some real formulas in which `numbers` is an expensive computation.

Maybe the formula should have been written like this:

```python
def function(self, simulation, period):
   booleans = simulation.calculate('booleans', period)
   numbers = self.zeros() \
        if all(booleans == False) \
        else simulation.calculate('numbers', period)
   return period, numbers * booleans
```

For those who know NumPy idioms what do you think?
@benjello @eraviart

issue_title: Le montant calculé de la prime d'activité n'est pas exact
issue_body:
J'ai l'impression que le façon dont la prime d'activité est calculée dans OpenFisca France est erronée. Par exemple, pour un célibataire à 1300 € nets par mois, OpenFisca France retourne 54,02 € de prime d'activité par mois (mois de décembre), tandis que selon le site service public le montant correspondant est de 36,37 € (https://www.service-public.fr/particuliers/vosdroits/F2882). 

Cet écart me semble dû au fait que dans OpenFisca le montant forfaitaire de la PA (parameter.prestations.minima_sociaux.rsa.montant_de_base_du_rsa.yaml) est de 536,36 € alors qu'il est de 526,25 € selon service-public.
Par ailleurs, dans OpenFisca, le montant forfaitaire du RSA (parameter.prestations.minima_sociaux.rmi.rmi.yaml) n'est pas à jour : il est maintenu à 535,17 € depuis le 1er septembre 2016, alors qu'il a été revalorisé à 545,48 € le 1er septembre 2017 (source : https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/eli/decret/2017/5/4/AFSA1707122D/jo).

Pour corriger cette erreur, il me semble qu'il faut fournir un travail plus important qu'une simple mise à jour des deux paramètres sus-cités, si on veut que l'ensemble fonctionne bien pour toutes les années de législation déjà codées.

Ci-dessous, le cas-type que j'ai essayé de simuler :
# Initialize test case
scenario.init_single_entity(
    period = 2017,
# Variable describing the individuals
    parent1 = dict(
        age = 30,
        salaire_net = 1300*12, # Annual basis
        ),
 # Variable describing the entity
    menage = dict(loyer = 5000, # Annual basis
            statut_occupation_logement = 1,
            taxe_habitation = -600,
            ),
    )

issue_title: TypeError: 'str' object is not callable
issue_body:
In python 3 with configparser i get the following error when trying to log a message:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/logging/__init__.py", line 980, in emit
    msg = self.format(record)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/logging/__init__.py", line 830, in format
    return fmt.format(record)
  File "/home/mafm/pycharm-35venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/logstash_formatter-0.5.15-py3.5.egg/logstash_formatter/__init__.py", line 172, in format
    return json.dumps(logr, default=self.json_default, cls=self.json_cls)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/json/__init__.py", line 237, in dumps
    **kw).encode(obj)
TypeError: 'str' object is not callable
```

It seems that _create_formatters in configparser will add a third argument (style) when initializing logstash formatter. This puts a string ("%") in stead of the json_cls. 
Might be a new behaviour of configparser?

Maybe the fix is as simple as changing the constructor functio (__init__) to
```
def __init__(self,
                 fmt=None,
                 datefmt=None,
                 style=None,
                 json_cls=None,
                 json_default=_default_json_default):

```
?

issue_title: Check if user state constraints make state unsatisfiable
issue_body:
> This issue was reported by @murmus. Thank you!

The `state.constrain()` API enables a user to add additional, arbitrary constraints onto a State. If these constraints actually make the state infeasible, this will later manifest as a `SolverException` when attempts to concretize symbolic data are made in this state (i.e. `state.solve_one`). As a side note, that exception will have an error message that is likely cryptic to the typical user (`Model is not available`).

If a state has been made infeasible by a user constraint, it likely means that original state was not the state their constraints were intended to be applied to.

proposed solution:
- on each constraint add, check whether added constraint make the state infeasible, if they do, abandon the state
- log this fact at the same log level as state forking, and other state related messages

alternative ideas:
- try to rollback the state and remove the constraints the user added and keep going?

issue_title: [wish] Make 'sfntedit' fail gracefully
issue_body: @readroberts Currently, when I use `sfntedit` to extract or delete a series of tables, and specify some tables that don't exist in the font (e.g. I say `sfntedit -d GPOS,GSUB font.ttf` and the font does not have `GSUB` but only `GPOS`), `sfntedit` issues an error `sfntedit [FATAL]: table missing (GSUB)` and does not perform the operation at all. It would be useful to have an option `-i` (ignore errors) that would ignore these situations and still perform the operations on the other tables which do exist. I don't know enough C to patch `sfntedit` myself but I think it should be a relatively minor but useful thing.

issue_body:
From https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pull/4695#discussion_r159756524:

I wrote: 
> I still cannot see how it is correct to scribble on the message objects defined outside spanner: it hides the actual source location, and worse, is likely to get stomped on by every one of our modules which uses it.
> 
> E.g.:

```python
>>> from google.longrunning import operations_pb2
>>> operations_pb2.CancelOperationRequest.__module__
'google.longrunning.operations_pb2'
>>> import google.cloud.spanner_admin_instance_v1.types
>>> operations_pb2.CancelOperationRequest.__module__
'google.cloud.spanner_admin_instance_v1.types'
>>> import google.cloud.spanner_admin_database_v1.types
>>> operations_pb2.CancelOperationRequest.__module__
'google.cloud.spanner_admin_database_v1.types'
>>> import google.cloud.language_v1beta2.types
>>> operations_pb2.CancelOperationRequest.__module__
'google.cloud.language_v1beta2.types'
```

@dhermes responded:

> I am with @tseaver here.

@lukesneeringer responded:

> I agree that this is a problem in theory. I am less convinced that it is a problem in practice.
>
> The key problem that this solves is with documentation: There is no good way (as far as I can tell) to tell Sphinx to present the message objects at a sane location other than doing what I did.
>
> I am all for an alternative method that gets a similar result. Essentially what I want is:
>
> - The message classes must be documented, and presented as part of the overall package.
> - The documentation should not make the user think about _pb2 junk.
> - Users should not have to think about the internal proto sorting.
>
> Basically, if there is some good way to have Sphinx present the modules in the location where we want to teach users to import them from, then I am completely fine with doing that in favor of what I have now.
>
> (I am also fine with being told that my goals above should be non-goals, with supporting argumentation of course.)

I replied:

> Until we are generating Sphinx docs from post-repo-split world, given the "last one wins" import race I demonstrated above, I don't believe your current solution actually means what you think it means. The messages will be generated with whatever module happened to be imported last as their faux location.
>
> >  The documentation should not make the user think about _pb2 junk.
>
> I would argue that this goal is the source of the breakage, and should be removed. For instance, users who look at docs for stdlib modules are not confused by references to top-level imports from other modules. Knowing that the message comes from proto-generated code (and which proto to examine, if need be) is actually claritying, IMNSHO.
>
> Even if we agreed that hiding the _pb2 was a good thing for messages defined inside e.g., google.cloud.spanner, it cannot be the Right Thing (TM) to pretend that stuff like LRO, Empty, etc. are defined there.

issue_title: bigquery.ArrayQueryParameter should be able to accept structs as elements of the array
issue_body:
I [commented](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pull/2901#discussion_r94066868) that setting the types on array query parameters needs to be recursive since the array could contain structs. Note that [arrays cannot contain arrays](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/data-types#array-type) (the workaround is to contain a struct which can then contain an array).

It's not clear how one would do this with the current `ArrayQueryParameter` constructor.

```
bigquery.ArrayQueryParameter(
    name='myarray', 
    type='STRUCT',  # Note: this isn't enough information to construct the type object.
    values=?  # What would go here? Dictionaries? That doesn't match other uses of StructQueryParameter.
)
```

Maybe we need to change how array query parameters are constructed? Maybe it should look more like the StructQueryParameter constructor?

```
bigquery.ArrayQueryParameter(name, *sub_params)
```

It'd be a pain for scalar values and the names would have to be ignored on the `sub_params` if provided, but it's more flexible.

*An aside*

*I'd actually love if we could get rid of all the `XQueryParameter` classes and use lists, dicts, and ints, floats, etc. But I see that it might be ambiguous for [some types](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/data-types) especially `DATETIME` (no time zone) vs `TIMESTAMP` (stored as UTC time).*

issue_title: Can't import `__version__` from `ppcommon` on `pip` install of `pathos`

issue_title: libsass miscompiles breadcrumb content
issue_body:
Under the environment:
```
sassc: 3.2.1
libsass: 3.2.4
sass2scss: 1.0.3
```
the SCSS in question:
```scss
content: "#{$breadcrumb-separator}\00a0";
```
is getting compiled to:
```css
content: "/\\00a0";
```
which is incorrect.
By comparison, Ruby Sass compiles this code to:
```css
content: "/ ";
```
which is correct (albeit not ASCII).

This is due to a bug in libsass: https://github.com/sass/libsass/issues/1115

The questions are (a) should bootstrap-sass try to workaround this? (b) if yes, how? (c) if yes, since this only affects libsass, do we make the change in bootstrap-sass, or in upstream Bootstrap?
CC: @twbs/sass

issue_title: ApiGateway::DomainName CertificateArn requirement prevents deployment
issue_body:
Issue: ApiGateway::DomainName requires CertificateArn.
The requirement ApiGateway:DomainName:CertificateArn  needs to be removed in-order to create an ApiGateway:DomainName w/ssl for an ApiGateway in REGION mode

Use-case:
API Gateway as a CloudFront origin.

In-order to implement this use-case, you need to deploy the following
API Gateway – REGION Deployment
ACM – generated in same region as the API gateway for the custom domain
AMC – Generated in us-east-1 For Cloudfront
ApyGateway:Domain w/RegionalCertificateArn
ApiGateway:BasePathMapping 


I’ve implemented the fix, ran tests and created a PR

https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/pull/996

Thanks!

-JH

issue_title: Add documentation on ivy (read) credentials
issue_body:
Overall I find the Ivy documentation difficult to find. I believe Ivy deserves its own page within the `JVM` section.
Within that page it would be nice to add a paragraph on configuring ivy for an enterprise repository that requires credentials - this info is somewhat available in the `Publishing Artifacts` page, but it isn't so obvious.
To achieve that I've setup pants this way:

* in `./pants.ini`

  ```ini
[ivy]
ivy_settings: build-support/ivy/ivysettings.xml 
```

* in `./build-support/ivy/ivysettings.xml`

  ```xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<ivysettings>
    <settings defaultResolver="maven"/>

    <properties file="${user.home}/.config/ivy/my-project-credentials.properties"/>

    <credentials host="maven.backuity.com"
                 realm="Sonatype Nexus Repository Manager"
                 username="${my-project.login}"
                 passwd="${my-project.password}"/>

    <include url="${ivy.default.settings.dir}/ivysettings-public.xml"/>
    <include url="${ivy.default.settings.dir}/ivysettings-shared.xml"/>
    <include url="${ivy.default.settings.dir}/ivysettings-local.xml"/>
    <include url="${ivy.default.settings.dir}/ivysettings-main-chain.xml"/>

    <resolvers>
        <chain name="maven" returnFirst="true">
            <ibiblio name="my-project" m2compatible="true"
                     root="https://maven.my-project.com/service/local/repositories/releases/content/"/>
            <!-- In this setup maven.my-project.com does not mirror maven central, it merely host
                 my-project artifacts. We'll need to add `main` (maven central) and `local` to fetch
                 the remaining artifacts.  
             -->
            <resolver ref="main"/>
            <resolver ref="local"/>
        </chain>
    </resolvers>
</ivysettings>
```

* in `${user.home}/.config/ivy/my-project-credentials.properties`

  ```properties
my-project.login=my-login
my-project.password=my-password
```

issue_title: Race condition when multiple gunicorn worker download models with same name
issue_body:
In our docker container, when multiple gunicorn worker download same models from S3 to disk, there will be read-write race condition because models are with the same name.

My proposal to fix it is adding a new optional argument: extract-to (Recommendation?) where the model will be saved and extracted to. 
Then we can create directory including gunicorn worker pid so models are separate across workers. It won't affect our current local serving flow but just requires some doc changes.
This argument would also be helpful if someone want to extract the model in different location rather than cwd.

issue_title: Dealing with categorical variables to predict new values in the input feature
issue_body: In sklearn pipeline we are able to create transformations for categorical variables via cat codes. Cat codes transformation is able to deal with new values while predicting, where as Label Encoder in sklearn2pmml isnt able to predict the new values, which were not available during training. My model can have new values for a column as an input feature while testing which would be a problem if we are using Label Encoder. Can you suggest a way how to deal with the PMML with the encoding, either in python or in JPMML side.

issue_title: Autocomplete does not allow filtering by administrative_area
issue_body:
[This line of documentation](https://github.com/googlemaps/google-maps-services-python/blob/69d80691bf0b262fbb5a18593891a01183dc29e3/googlemaps/places.py#L335) claims that one can filter autocomplete results by an 'administrative_area'. Actually doing so, however, causes a 500 error. It seems that the API doesn't accept anything but 'country' as a property of the components parameter.

Preferred solution: squash [this bug](https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/35822067). But it's a P4 :(

Alternatively: just update the docs

issue_title: Unsubscribe may block
issue_body:
I have encountered an issue cleaning up subscriptions when switching between networks.

I'm using soco on a phone. And when switching between wifi and 3G (or wifi and wifi), I clean old subscriptions before maybe making new ones.

Normally unsubscribe works fine. But in this case, it blocks for a long time. Presumably it is confused because all of a sudden it's on a new network with different ips and such.

I would not expect this behavior, so I'm assuming it's a bug. But it's also very possible I am driving soco wrong. Is there a best-practice for resetting state when switching networks?

issue_title: set returned by `discover()` can mutate
issue_body:
Recently, my program failed with the following error:

```none
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./sonos-button.py", line 85, in <module>
    main()
  File "./sonos-button.py", line 52, in main
    coordinators = find_coordinators(zones)
  File "./sonos-button.py", line 79, in find_coordinators
    for zone in zones:
RuntimeError: Set changed size during iteration
```

This appears to have happened because `soco.discovery.discover()` does not return a static set, but rather [returns a reference to a set managed by a zone object](https://github.com/SoCo/SoCo/blob/22e787dad105ddb2814dfd4cd1d4d76e2349dc4a/soco/discovery.py#L177). This means that when the zone changes, the set's contents can change as well. And if the set's contents change in a separate thread due to an event, the above error can happen.

My suggestion would be to either change the documentation to make it clear that the returned set is dynamic and should be copied before iteration, or return a copy of the zone's set. (I have a slight preference for the documentation change since having a dynamic set is handy.)

issue_title: charm-version fails when installed via snap
issue_body:
## Checklist

 - [X] Confirmed this is an issue with charm-tools, not charmstore-client
 - [X] Provide versions of tools used
 - [X] Described the feature or ways to replicate the issue

## What version am I running?

I ran the following command: `charm version` and got the following ouput:

```
charm error
charm-tools 2.1.2
```

Output of `snap list charm`:

```
Name   Version  Rev  Developer  Notes
charm  2.2      5    charms     -
```

I am using: Ubuntu Xenial

## Issue/Feature

### I expect/expected the following

`charm-version` should correct report the version of the snap-installed `charm` command as well as the apt-installed version.

### What I got

It instead reports `charm error`.

This is due to [charmtools.version.charm_version](https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/blob/master/charmtools/version.py#L19) only trying apt to find the version.  This could fail, or worse, report an incorrect version.

It should probably detect whether `/snap/bin/charm` or `/usr/bin/charm` is being used (parent process inspection? or just trying one and falling back to the other, I guess) and consult the appropriate system for the version.

issue_title: A Makefile in the layer tramples the generated Makefile in the built charm
issue_body:
## Checklist

 - [X] Confirmed this is an issue with charm-tools, not charmstore-client
 - [X] Provide versions of tools used
 - [X] Described the feature or ways to replicate the issue

## What version am I running?

I ran the following command: `charm version` and got the following ouput:

```
$ venv/bin/charm-version 
charm unavailable # The charm command is not installed by `venv/bin/pip install charm-tools`
charm-tools 2.1.4
```

I am using: Ubuntu Xenial 16.04.1

## Issue/Feature

### I expect/expected the following

I expected the the Makefile in the layered charm (which ought to govern the layered charm only) not trample the makefile generated during `charm build`.  At the very least, something like `Warning: not generating Makefile for <charm>` would be nice.

### What I got

We use a Makefile and a virtualenv to pin the version of charm-tools to a working version.  When running `charm build`, that Makefile takes precedence and the generated Makefile (which contains the targets for running tests, lint, etc) is not written, and instead I only have the targets required for running `charm build`.

neighbor 172.27.10.202 {         
      router-id 172.27.10.120;
      local-address 172.27.10.120;
      local-as 1;
      peer-as 1;
      hold-time 60;
      group-updates;
      family {
             ipv4 unicast;
             ipv4 nlri-mpls;
             ipv4 flow;
      }
      capability {
             graceful-restart;
             route-refresh enable;
      }

}

neighbor 172.27.10.77 {
      router-id 172.27.10.120;
      local-address 172.27.10.120;
      local-as 1;
      peer-as 1;
      hold-time 60;
      family {
             ipv4 unicast;
             ipv4 flow;
      }

}


      process abc{
          run python /home/lab/exabgp/http-api.py;
          encoder json;
      }            

lab@ubuntu-120:~/exabgp$

```

<!--- You can also use gist.github.com links for larger files -->

##### SUMMARY
<!--- Explain the problem briefly -->
I am testing flowspec with exabgp, and updating routes by using http api. I use the curl form.
exabgp works fine when using accept/discard options, for example:
curl --form "command=neighbor 172.27.10.77 announce flow route source 10.30.2.0/24 destination 210.210.210.0/24 accept" http://localhost:5001/neighbor 172.27.10.77 announce flow route source 10.30.2.0/24 destination 210.210.210.0/24 accept

But exabgp will tear down neighbor when using redirect option, for example:
curl --form "command=neighbor 172.27.10.77 announce flow route source 10.30.2.0/24 destination 210.210.210.0/24 redirect 65000:1" http://localhost:5001/neighbor 172.27.10.77 announce flow route source 10.30.2.0/24 destination 210.210.210.0/24 redirect 65000:1

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
<!---
For bugs, please if possible, please run with the "-d" option and provide the full output,
If not possible, please provide the full fault section as reported by ExaBGP
-->


##### EXPECTED RESULTS
<!--- What did you expect to happen when running the steps above? -->
I expect exabgp can update routes to peer when using redirect option.

##### ACTUAL RESULTS
<!--- What actually happened? -->
exabgp tears down it's peer and try to reopen 
##### IMPORTANCE
<!-- Please let us know if the issue is affecting you in a production environment -->

issue_title: Install via Python Wheels
issue_body: create a universal wheel distribution

issue_title: Add new --pid option for host pid namespace
issue_body:
In Docker 1.5 comes a new option of `--pid`, which allows the container to see all the host's PID namespace. Here is mentioned in the 1.5 changelog:

https://github.com/docker/docker/commit/a3934504f905e29187c895a44ddf5ed8ce874414#diff-4ac32a78649ca5bdd8e0ba38b7006a1eR29

And here the PR source code, which has been merged ready for 1.5:

https://github.com/docker/docker/pull/10080/files

If we can please have `--pid` option added to docker-py, so that fig can also be able to set it? Requesting this feature as a user, to run jpetazzo's pipework in fig. It definately needs --pid option (I have checked).

It would be a bonus if can be ready in time before next fig releases, either 1.10 or 1.20.
Many thanks.

issue_title: Support default parameter values in generated APIs
issue_body:
We want some of the generated methods to have default values.
We need to add support for this to our code generator through functions_addons.py.

Examples:

`ViStatus _VI_FUNC niDMM_InitWithOptions(ViRsrc resourceName, ViBoolean IDQuery, ViBoolean   resetDevice, ViString optionsString, ViSession *newVi);
`
IDQuery, resetDevice could have defaults. Maybe a bad example because we are going to handcode constructors anyway.

```
ViStatus _VI_FUNC niDMM_GetDevTemp(
   ViSession   vi,
   ViString    reserved,
   ViReal64    *temperature);
```
Reserved should be omitted or at least defaulted to something for the Python user.

There may be other examples.

issue_title: Rename `raw` keyword `delayed`
issue_body:
With #138 fixed, the operator `raw` now has 2 roles:

1. Input contents that will otherwise create a conflict in DSC interface.
2. Input a parameter whose value depends on another one. For example:

```
n: 3
m: raw(n * 2)
```

will make sure that `m` is evaluated after other parameters that do not have `raw` in them.

So I think a more appropriate name for `raw` is perhaps `delayed`? Any other suggestions?

issue_title: Design Doc: Support Private Registries Behind Auth
issue_body:
Goal: 
To support enterprise stacks without the need for `git push deis` which is often slow in most normal network conditions.

Code Changes:
Client - implement flag -f --forward-credentials
  - this can utilize a users docker login, to forward credentials from ~/.docker/auth.json
  - this would be familiar to users of other schedulers


Builder - go-dockerclient and docker remote api support the x-registry-auth and related headers.
Our builder should leverage theses with the forwarded credentials

By always forwarding credentials we improve security dramatically by not storing state in the cluster.

Testing:
  Unit tests should be implemented at the builder level for param validation.
  Functional tests should test the relationship between the client and the builder service.


Any suggestions?

issue_title: Support for ^Bookmark syntax
issue_body:
@tuecke

> Why not support the same ^bookmark functionality in the new CLI? That was the result of a LOT of discussion in the ssh CLI, and is much more user friendly than your proposed solution.

Here's what I remember from when we last talked about the ^Bookmark syntax to support it:
- [ ] research how to correctly handle escaping, including spaces, circumflexes, slashes, colons
  - [ ] do we provide a helper for script writers to escape human input?
- [ ] research how this works when combined with paths, including issues with relative paths
- [ ] research how to implement this as an argument type in the Click framework such that it is resolved to an endpoint UUID/path pair once the body of the function fires
- [ ] decide if we keep the `bookmark locate` method (this method requires no escaping for someone handling user input, for example, whereas the ^Bookmark method would)

issue_title: Add example/documentation how to use tsfresh for time series forecasting
Segfault when double arrow-fetching result
issue_body:
After calling `fetcharrow` multiple times, the python process crashing when calling `to_pandas`. A minimal example is:

```python
c = conn.cursor()
c.execute('select * from data')

c.fetchallarrow()
c.fetchallarrow().to_pandas()
```

To my surpirse, it seems the call to `to_pandas` is required for the crash to happen. Running the following code does not result in a crash:

```python
c = conn.cursor()
c.execute('select * from data')

print(len(c.fetchallarrow()))
print(len(c.fetchallarrow()))
print(len(c.fetchallarrow()))
# output:
# 100000
# 0
# 0
```

The table `test_data` was created with

```python
df = pd.DataFrame({'i': np.random.randint(0, 128, size=100_000) })
df.to_sql('test_data', engine)
```

While I assume calling `fetchallarrow()` multiple times is not supported, my preference would be to either return an empty dataframe or to raise a python exception.

CC. @xhochy

issue_title: Simple model of itsdb tables and rows
issue_body:
ItsdbProfile.read_table() should perhaps return a basic ItsdbTable instance, which might just be a wrapper around a list. Similarly, it could be a list of ItsdbRows, which wraps another list (not a dict). Then functions like select() could be on these classes.

One benefit, besides a more friendly API, is that joined tables can be more manageable, with names, easier column selection, etc.

issue_title: Basic authentication broken: login:password components from channel urls lost by prioritize_channels()
issue_body:
With conda 4.2.9,
running `conda search xxx`, or `conda install xxx` on a channel (`https://user:password@server/channels`) protected by basic authentication fails `with HTTPError: 401 Client Error: Unauthorized for url: https://server/channels/osx-64/repodata.json: https://server/channels/osx-64/`

For what I understand, `user:password` components are lost by 
```
def prioritize_channels(channels):
    # ('https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/osx-64/', ('conda-forge', 1))
    result = odict()
    for q, chn in enumerate(channels):
        channel = Channel(chn)
        for url in channel.urls:
            if url in result:
                continue
            result[url] = channel.canonical_name, q
    return result
```
where
```
channel._raw_value = https://user:password@server/channels
result = OrderedDict([(u'https://server/channels/osx-64/', (u'https://server/channels', 0)), (u'https://server/channels/noarch/', (u'https://server/channels', 0))])
```

issue_title: Connection failure due to bad username/password fails to report reason
issue_body:
Upon attempting to connect with bad credentials, a ConnectionError is reported with the following information:

`````
pyeapi.eapilib.ConnectionError: unable to connect to eAPI
`````
There could be a few reasons for this, I would imaging, for example, the device not being configured / enabled for eAPI.

But the actual device response_content value in the underlying eapilib.py file, when debugged, provides the root cause:

````
(Pdb) pp(response_content)
'Unable to authenticate user: Bad username/password combination'
````

It would be very helpful if this information (response_content) could be bubbled up as an additional argument value to the ConnectionError.

Thank you!

issue_title: log_types.vhd - possible bug
issue_body: When compiling vunit/vhdl/logging/src/log_types.vhd, Modelsim gives a warning on line 25 "Range 1 to 0 is null". Looking at the source, line 25 declares a constant of type log_level_vector_t. The log_level_vector_t is a natural array. Natural is 0 to integer'HIGH, so line 25 should be log_level_vector_t(0 to 1), instead of "1 to 0". When I make the change, the compiler warning goes away.

issue_title: Curator failing to remove an index from an alias
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce 

You need install CURATOR 3.30 

Create an index
```
PUT /test_batch/test/1
{"name":"samir"}
```
add the index to the alias 

```
POST /_aliases
{
    "actions" : [
        { "add" : { "index" : "test_batch", "alias" : "test_alias" } }
    ]
}
```
and use curator to remove the index from the alias 
```
sudo curator --debug --host 127.0.0.1 alias --name 'test_alias' --remove indices --prefix 'test_batch'
```
it will fail with the follwing error 

```
2015-10-06 18:32:03,830 DEBUG     urllib3.connectionpool          _make_request:387  "HEAD /test_alias/_alias HTTP/1.1" 404 0
```
https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/


When discussing with @untergeek , we found that the issue only happened when using urllib3 version 1.12 . If we use the version 1.11 it work fine.

issue_title: Help: describe the [id, name, rg] clearly
issue_body:
It is a known issue general to all commands.  The help text shows no fields are mandatory, but in fact, people should supply either "resource group"+"name", or "resource id"
Though a little odd, but `(--resource-group --name | --ids)` communicate better.
```
Command
    az vm show

Arguments
    --api-version      : Client Api Version.  Default: 2016-03-30.
    --expand           : The expand expression to apply on the operation.
    --ids              : ID of resource.
    --name -n          : The name of the virtual machine.
    --resource-group -g: Name of resource group.

(env) D:\sdk\azure-cli>az vm show
usage: az vm show [-h] [--output {json,tsv,list,table}] [--verbose] [--debug]
                  [--query JMESPATH] [--expand EXPAND]
                  [--api-version API_VERSION]
                  [--ids RESOURCE_ID [RESOURCE_ID ...]]
                  [--resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME] [--name NAME]
az vm show: error: (--resource-group --name | --ids) are required
```

issue_title: `az resource group list -o table` is missing Status column
issue_body:
My workflow:
1. Run a bunch of Kubernetes deployments. Each gets its own resource group. So I might rack up a dozen or more an hour.
2. Get to a stopping point, or remember that you're using a bunch of resources.
3. `az resource group list -o table | grep colemick`
4. Manually eyeball the output and find resource groups that are not deleted, issue `az resource group delete --name=colemick-test-0532`.

I know that I could script this, but I did that once and almost immediately wound up shooting myself in the foot, even with a confirmation step.

Long story short: this was easy in azure-xplat-cli because the table output told me the status by default.

issue_title: Enable MSI authentication for BlockBlobService
issue_body:
Currently the class azure.storage.blob.BlockBlobService only supports storage key or Shared Access Token. 

However, storage itself supports MSI based authentication. Please enable support for using MSI to access storage blobs

issue_title: .git/hooks/pre-commit points to a dead symlink in a non-writeable directory => IOError
issue_body:
```
$ pre-commit install -f --install-hooks
An unexpected error has occurred: IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/home/.../.git/hooks/pre-commit'
$ cat ~/.pre-commit/pre-commit.log
An unexpected error has occurred: IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/home.../.git/hooks/pre-commit'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/.../.tox/pre-commit/lib/python2.6/site-packages/pre_commit/error_handler.py", line 34, in error_handler
    yield
  File "/home/.../.tox/pre-commit/lib/python2.6/site-packages/pre_commit/main.py", line 135, in main
    hook_type=args.hook_type,
  File "/home/.../.tox/pre-commit/lib/python2.6/site-packages/pre_commit/commands/install_uninstall.py", line 73, in install
    with io.open(hook_path, 'w') as pre_commit_file_obj:
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.6/io.py", line 231, in open
    closefd)
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.6/io.py", line 631, in __init__
    _fileio._FileIO.__init__(self, name, mode, closefd)
IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/home/.../.git/hooks/pre-commit'
```
https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit/blob/a97cb38b9a8bf72e55489d5ba3f038722491bd1d/pre_commit/commands/install_uninstall.py#L56

A workaround is to manually rm the dead symlink

CC @dkunitsk

issue_title: Older versions of git no longer supported
issue_body:
Since #714 I am no longer able to run `pre-commit` on RHEL7 hosts.  The version of `git` provided by the OS vendor does not support the `-C` option.

Was this intentional?  If not, would you consider re-adding support for older versions?

My environment:
```
$ cat /etc/redhat-release
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server release 7.3 (Maipo)

$ git --version
git version 1.8.3.1
```
I understand that version of `git` is 5+ years old, but it is the latest version provided by RedHat.  If nothing else, the documentation should state which version of `git` is required.

Thanks

issue_title: Recent janus releases on PyPI are missing sdists
issue_body: According to https://pypi.python.org/simple/janus/, there are only wheels, not tar.gz archives for recent (>= 0.2) releases of janus. This makes it impossible to install in an older packaging environment (missing wheel  support). Could sdist be uploaded on PyPI for recent releases?

issue_title: CookieError on invalid cookie names
issue_body:
## Long story short

aiohttp servers fail with 500 Internal error on requests where Cookie header is present and cookie name contains invalid symbols for cookie name. aiohttp uses SimpleCookie from python's http.cookies package. Although SimpleCookie follows standards and supposedly correctly rejects such cookie names, real world is much messier and such cookies are seen frequently.

For the reference, RFC2109 stated that cookie attribute (name) is a `token`:

    The two state management headers, Set-Cookie and Cookie, have common
    syntactic properties involving attribute-value pairs.  The following
    grammar uses the notation, and tokens DIGIT (decimal digits) and
    token (informally, a sequence of non-special, non-white space
    characters) from the HTTP/1.1 specification [RFC 2068] to describe
    their syntax.
    
    av-pairs        =       av-pair *(";" av-pair)
    av-pair         =       attr ["=" value]        ; optional value
    attr            =       token

RFC2616 defines `token` as:

    token          = 1*<any CHAR except CTLs or separators>
    separators     = "(" | ")" | "<" | ">" | "@"
                   | "," | ";" | ":" | "\" | <">
                   | "/" | "[" | "]" | "?" | "="
                   | "{" | "}" | SP | HT

So any cookie attribute containing one of `( ) < > @ , ; : \ " / [ ] ? = { } SP HT` is considered invalid by SimpleCookie. Django uses own cookie parsing because of similar reasons.

## Expected behaviour

Such invalid cookie should still be accepted as they are tolerated by browsers and most web servers.

## Actual behaviour

500 Internal server error caused by exception CookieError.

## Steps to reproduce

Given sample http server:

    from aiohttp import web
    
    async def handle(request):
        text = ""
        for k, v in request.cookies:
            text += '\n{}\t{}'.format(k, v)
        return web.Response(text=text)
    
    app = web.Application()
    app.router.add_get('/', handle)
    app.router.add_get('/{name}', handle)
    
    web.run_app(app)

When one executes `curl -H 'Cookie: ISAWPLB{DB45DF86-F806-407C-932C-D52A60E4019E}=x' -v http://localhost:8080/` against this server it receives 500 internal server error. On the server side a traceback is visible:

    Error handling request
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/aiohttp/helpers.py", line 554, in __get__
        return inst._cache[self.name]
    KeyError: 'cookies'
    
    During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
    
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/aiohttp/web_protocol.py", line 410, in start
        resp = yield from self._request_handler(request)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/aiohttp/web.py", line 325, in _handle
        resp = yield from handler(request)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/aiohttp/web_middlewares.py", line 93, in impl
        return (yield from handler(request))
      File "./test.py", line 7, in handle
        for k, v in request.cookies:
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/aiohttp/helpers.py", line 556, in __get__
        val = self.wrapped(inst)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/aiohttp/web_request.py", line 413, in cookies
        parsed = SimpleCookie(raw)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.5/http/cookies.py", line 507, in __init__
        self.load(input)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.5/http/cookies.py", line 556, in load
        self.__parse_string(rawdata)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.5/http/cookies.py", line 620, in __parse_string
        self.__set(key, rval, cval)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.5/http/cookies.py", line 512, in __set
        M.set(key, real_value, coded_value)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.5/http/cookies.py", line 380, in set
        raise CookieError('Illegal key %r' % (key,))
    http.cookies.CookieError: Illegal key 'ISAWPLB{DB45DF86-F806-407C-932C-D52A60E4019E}'

## Your environment

Tested on Ubuntu 16.04, aiohttp version 2.3.9 installed through pip.

issue_title: Add easier way to modify OutputArea.display_order
Proposal for fixing mirroring issues in Jupyter Notebook
issue_body:
## Jupyter Notebook
Arabic Data Handling

### Overview
Mirroring is essential in case the application interface is translated to any language with a Bidirectional script (e.g. Arabic, Hebrew). The Arabic and Hebrew script natural Global Orientation is from right-to-left, hence, the native speakers of these languages expect to see the GUI layout mirrored so that the whole GUI can be read from right-to left as well. This includes viewing menu buttons on the right, the navigation tree on the right, and indentation to the left. GUI mirroring is necessary for any application which has GUIs meant to be translated to a language based on bidi (i.e. Bidirectional) scripts such as Arabic and Hebrew.


## High-level Design
Whenever the Arabic or Hebrew translation is loaded, mirroring of the UI will occur, this may depend on web browser setting or UI language setting.


### Preferences
No special preference needed for mirroring

### Terminology and Acronyms
•	LTR: Left to Right
•	RTL: Right to Left
•	GUI Mirroring: Change the Graphical User Interface directionality from a Left to Right orientation to a Right to Left orientation. (The effect is a horizontal position swapping of the GUI elements)

### Mirroring Issues
In this section, we highlight mirroring issues found when enforcing right directionality (RTL) on Jupyter Notebook.

### 1- Main Page :
Comparing the below two images, it is apparent that mirroring issues with the editor. The following is a list of issues found:
•	Header logo should be on the right.
•	Tabbed items should be on the right (menu tabs)
•	Toolbar items should be swapped 

![1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651141/fcfb2090-9a0b-11e6-91da-bfae1754d91e.png)
Figure:1 Main Page in RTL Mode

![2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651161/0d775e0c-9a0c-11e6-8b16-ce857909a2f7.png)
Figure:2 Main Page in LTR Mode


### 2- Dropdown Menu:
•	Menu items should be aligned right.
•	Tree-selector buttons should be swapped.
The following images will highlight these issues.

![3](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651209/3d212a02-9a0c-11e6-8ae5-fca7cceab951.png) 
Figure:3 Dropdown Menu in RTL mode  


![4](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651234/5435024a-9a0c-11e6-9829-7833674c4038.png)
Figure:4 Dropdown Menu in LTR mode


### 3- Dialogues: 
Dialogues had several mirroring issues:
•	Close button should be placed on the left.
•	Action buttons should be placed on the left.

   The following images will highlight these issues.

![5](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651257/65a66de8-9a0c-11e6-961a-62b62537507e.png)
Figure 5: Dialogues in RTL mode


![6](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651265/70ed660c-9a0c-11e6-83cd-6b29f5072482.png)
Figure 6: Dialogues in LTR mode


### 4- Notebooks:  
It has several mirroring issue:
•	Code editor: missing parts in the line editor.
•	Dropdown menu & sub menus should be on the right.
•	Menu bar blocks should be swapped.
The following images will highlight these issues.

![7](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651280/847a6972-9a0c-11e6-8974-52d450b34aa9.png)
Figure 7: Notebook in RTL mode.


![8](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19651289/8f2e59d2-9a0c-11e6-8e65-fb0fe7e5ad1a.png)
Figure 8: Notebook in LTR mode.


## Low Level Design
In this section, the detailed design proposed will be explained.

### Document Direction
Add an attribute named ‘dir’ with value ‘rtl’ to an element, and all elements underneath will be
rendered right-to-left. For the sake of simplicity, let us assume we put this attribute on the body
element.

Code 1: 
by creating a function to check the ui language & change the dir attribute if it's RTL language on the body in a separate script tag inside **page.html**

![1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19652056/aff6b01c-9a0f-11e6-9385-0510f254e1a2.png)


### CSS Style
The browser doesn’t render right-to-left perfectly, and a lot of the remaining issues can be solved using CSS. For this, we will use “CSS Overriding” mirroring approach to fix the CSS classes. We will use CSS attributes to enforce the change in the CSS attributes.
We will update the CSS files by overriding them with the “dir” attribute and update the properties as
well in the same style sheet that contains the original style (LTR-mode) .

Code 2:
by overriding these styles in:
 
- **tree.less :**

![3](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19652104/e1c94d02-9a0f-11e6-88f6-baa5c0056b97.png)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
![4](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19652106/e1da3888-9a0f-11e6-9248-e5c79b09dd9c.png)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
![5](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19652105/e1d05c0a-9a0f-11e6-84d1-04cf4dbdddb3.png)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

- **page.less :**


![2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19652107/e1db1398-9a0f-11e6-941d-a435f77050f0.png)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Results
The screenshot shows the results of applying the design proposed in this report.

![fixed](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20149645/19652123/f1773926-9a0f-11e6-85d6-8ae61c30b633.png)

issue_title: Problem: [origins] Validation regex for "origin" or "referrer" matches untrusted domain.
issue_body:
Hello. I'm curious the question about CORS

```nginx
if ($http_origin ~* ((^https://www\.yandex\.ru)|(^https://ya\.ru)/)) {
	add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' "$http_origin";
	add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials' 'true';
}
```

The Host Header ABNF is as the following [whatwg](https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#origin-header) or [rfc6454](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6454#section-7):
```
Origin                           = origin-or-null

origin-or-null              = origin / %x6E.75.6C.6C ; "null", case-sensitive
origin                           = scheme "://" host [ ":" port ]
```

The origin does not reveal a path. So use regex pattern `^` and `$` is enough in my option. Are there any browser will include the path when send a ajax?

issue_title: Filenames cannot start with a colon
issue_body:
There's an issue with `GitSession.list_tree_entries` due to a quirk in how `git ls-tree` processes its paths (presumably common logic in how git handles its path specifications). This prevents paths from starting with a colon (git often separates the committish/tree from a path that way, so that may be the issue).

Example:
```
$ ls
:cht.sh  cht.sh
$ git ls-tree HEAD cht.sh :cht.sh
100644 blob e69de29bb2d1d6434b8b29ae775ad8c2e48c5391    cht.sh
$ git ls-tree HEAD ./cht.sh ./:cht.sh
100644 blob e69de29bb2d1d6434b8b29ae775ad8c2e48c5391    :cht.sh
100644 blob e69de29bb2d1d6434b8b29ae775ad8c2e48c5391    cht.sh
```

This causes problems for the particular case of using peru to pull in https://github.com/chubin/cheat.sh, as its url for installation is https://cht.sh/:cht.sh.

Afaict we can resolve this issue by prefixing all pathnames with `./` to explicitly specify that it's relative to pwd, much the way it's safest to do the same when writing shell scripts (i.e. `canonical_path = os.path.join('.', pathlib.PurePosixPath(path))`,  or we can add a specific workaround to do this only when the path begins with `:`.

issue_title: Funkiness when "text=auto" is in .gitattributes
issue_body:
I think I found an interesting edge-case kind of thing here... not sure if it's a problem that Peru can solve for itself, or if it's a weird Git thing.

If a Git repo is set to normalize line-endings (`core.autocrlf` in Git's config.), Peru thinks thinks "Imported files have been modified" if line-endings differ between files in Peru's cache and the repo's working directory.

To reproduce:

 1. Have a Git repo with these in it:
    - a `peru.yaml` file with one or more modules have contents with different line-ending characters.
    - a `.gitattributes` file with [`* text=auto`](https://help.github.com/articles/dealing-with-line-endings/#per-repository-settings) and/or [a matching global git config](https://help.github.com/articles/dealing-with-line-endings/#global-settings-for-line-endings).
 1. Run `peru sync`.

  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/peru/cache.py", line 75, in _git
    raise self.GitError(command, output, process.returncode)
peru.cache.GitError: git command "git --git-dir=<working-dir>.peru/cache/trees --work-tree=<working-dir> checkout-index --all" returned error code 128:
<list of files>
```

Other bits:

 * All of this goes away and Peru runs normally when the `core.autocrlf` setting is completely unset.
 * This also happens with `peru reup`, both the safe-fail because of perceived changes in the working dir, and the traceback when running with `--force`.

I was able to confirm that it's line-ending shenanigans that's getting Peru's Git guts tripped up. Despite the errors in the traceback, it looks like Peru is still getting files written out to the file system correctly. I _think_. Using Git to track the imported files themselves, the only changes to these files that Git sees are line-endings; do a `git stash && git stash pop` and the working directory will be clean again. Or, if doing a `peru reup --force`, the list of changed files will drop from "lots and lots" to only `peru.yaml` and whatever few files have changed between the two `rev`s.

issue_title: TODO
issue_body:
This issue consolidates in a single location todo that were originally listed in #36 and #38 (later superseded by #43):

From #36 and #38:
- [x] Extend testing coverage to include the new apps and new features.
- [x] Currently the only way to add to the Cython module search path for `add_cython_target` is to use the outdated `include_directories` CMake command.  Ideally, we would be able to use `target_include_directories` or something similar.  This may involve the creation of an actual CMake target. This is now tracked in https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/issues/243
- [x] There is a bug when trying to specify the desired CMake build type via the `--build-type` option in scikit-build.  It seems that some sections of the code are looking for `CMAKE_BUILD_DIR/CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE` as the ultimate location of the cmake build directory.  I think handling multiple builds in one tree is desirable and we should probably extend this handling to the other temporary staging directories, but right now, these improvements were not expecting this.  We'll need to go through the various code sections and make sure everyone's on the same page.
- [x] Refactor the custom distutils command subclasses so that they are accessible for other packages -- in case they want to work around us just as we did distutils.
- [x] Restore the default handling of generic data files so that it matches up with distutils' default behavior.  To handle stand-alone extension modules, we'll need to add extra handling logic for the `py_modules` argument; we would list these modules under `py_modules`, and leave it to scikit-build to recognize them and place them accordingly.
- [x] Add a final check to the manifest processing loop so that skbuild refuses to handle files installed outside of the project root.
- [x] Add option to the `add_cython_target` function to override the variable name used to store the location to the generated source file.
- [x] Remove TODO note in `add_cython_target` regarding C vs C++ output.  So far, no problems have been encountered one way or the other during development and testing.
- [x] Remove the FindDebugger.cmake file (used just for debugging cmake targets).
- [x] Update the `python_modules_header` function so that the `FORWARD_DECL_MODULES_VAR` option is renamed to `FORWARD_DECL_MODULES_LIST` and that the list in question is passed by value.
- [x] Add option to the `python_modules_header` function to override the variable names used to store the locations of the generated header file as well as that of the directory containing it.
- [x] Clean up tower-of-babel boost module header files.  Use `tbabel_boost_common.h` to minimize code duplication.
- [x] Projects that attempt to CMake-install files outside of its designated project installation root should fail to install.
  - [x] Ideally, they should fail prior to the CMake-install step (i.e.: before any problematic files are actually installed).  This will need code to determine the would-be files without the aid of the install manifest.
- [x] Each of the sample projects should have their own tests to at least ensure that they build correctly, and that any produced applications run without issue.
- [x] Add tests for pen2_cython
- [x] Add tests for tower-of-babel
- [x] There are multiple bugs in the way command line arguments are processed and forwarded in scikit-build.  It's not clear if they were introduced by these changes, or if they predate them.
  - [x] On a related note, I tried to pass options directly to CMake through scikit-build using the `--` argument seperator.  Scikit-build is currently written to expect up to three sections of such options: the first for scikit-build options, the second for CMake config options, and the third for options to the build step.  However, since CMake is used to drive the build step, options meant for the actual build tool (e.g.: make) must be separated by another `--` separator.  Currently scikit-build fails to properly handle this fourth separator.

The BAM->FASTQ conversion using `samtools fastq -1 /data/output_1.fastq -2 /data/output_2.fastq -0 /data/output_0.fastq /data/mybam.bam` resulted in fastq files which were not sorted, so pairs did not line up between the two files.

I'm testing now sorting them using:
```
zcat LP6005441-DNA_A01_1.fastq.gz | paste - - - - | sort -k1,1 -S 10G -T temp/ |  tr '\t' '\n' | gzip > /sorted/LP6005441-DNA_A01_1.fastq.gz 
```
and if successful will run this sorting as a toil job, and update original BAM->FASTQ conversion code to sort by default.
I'll provide an example pair of files from a single sample ASAP for use in testing.

issue_title: Rejigger sorting in ADAM/GATK pipeline

issue_title: No Attribute NullHandler when running pytest -n
issue_body:
I just figured out a very obscure issue that manifests itself when you try to use pytest-xdist. I doubt that it can really be considered a bug, but I thought I would record it here for posterity, in case another poor soul runs into this problem.

### TLDR

There is a logging module in PyPi: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/logging, but actually logging is part of the stdlib.

If you have this module installed, pytest-xdist somehow chokes while running tests, in a surprising way that gives you the impression that xdist is broken.

### Sample Project

``` bash
➜ tree -I venv
.
└── tests
    │
    └── test_stuff.py

➜ pip freeze
apipkg==1.4
backoff==1.3.1
execnet==1.4.1
logging==0.4.9.6
py==1.4.31
pytest==3.0.3
pytest-xdist==1.15.0
wheel==0.24.0
```

tests/test_stuff.py:
```python
import unittest
import backoff

class TestFoo(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_foo(self):
        print 'ok'
        assert True
```

```
➜ py.test tests 
============================================================================== test session starts ===============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.5, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/jonathanlafleche/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug, inifile: 
plugins: xdist-1.15.0
collected 1 items 

tests/test_stuff.py .

============================================================================ 1 passed in 0.02 seconds ============================================================================
```

```
➜ py.test -n 5 tests
============================================================================== test session starts ===============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.5, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/jonathanlafleche/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug, inifile: 
plugins: xdist-1.15.0
gw0 [0] / gw1 [0] / gw2 [0] / gw3 [0] / gw4 [0]
scheduling tests via LoadScheduling

===================================================================================== ERRORS =====================================================================================
______________________________________________________________________ ERROR collecting tests/test_stuff.py ______________________________________________________________________
tests/test_stuff.py:2: in <module>
    import backoff
venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/backoff.py:35: in <module>
    logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())  # pragma: no cover
E   AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'NullHandler'
============================================================================ 1 error in 0.70 seconds =============================================================================
(venv)jonathanlafleche@MAC-10001148 in ~/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug  
```

After bashing my head against the keyboard for _a while_, I figured it out:
```
pip uninstall logging
➜ py.test -n 5 tests   
============================================================================== test session starts ===============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.5, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/jonathanlafleche/workspace/fuck/pytest-bug, inifile: 
plugins: xdist-1.15.0
gw0 [1] / gw1 [1] / gw2 [1] / gw3 [1] / gw4 [1]
scheduling tests via LoadScheduling
.
============================================================================ 1 passed in 0.78 seconds ============================================================================
```

issue_title: Tests failing on Python 3.3
issue_body:
The tests seem to be failing on Python 3.3 due to a wheel incompatibility.

My best guess for what is happening is that `wheel` dropped support for Python 3.3 and something in the testing pipeline (only seems to be happening on Travis as far as I can tell) is using an old version of `pip`.

My suspicion is that Travis has some code that creates a `virtualenv`, and it does so with an un-upgraded version of `pip`, which breaks on Python 3.3. It might be best to kick this over to Travis's github issues and see if we can get it fixed there. Otherwise we'll probably have to modify the way Travis creates its virtualenvs.

In the end we'll just drop Python 3.3 support soon enough, but I'd like to make sure I can do this in a pinch if need be and I don't like the idea of being forced to drop Python 3.3 by a misconfigured CI. Generally I don't drop support for a version of Python unless it's burdensome to continue supporting it in the code itself (i.e. a descriptive approach to version support). That hasn't happened for Python 3.3 yet.

issue_title: Parse views docstrings for summary/description
issue_body:
Following up on a discussion in flask-rest-api: https://github.com/Nobatek/flask-rest-api/pull/3.

A contributor suggested to parse the docstrings for `summary`. I think we can go further and also get `description`:

```py
def my_view(...):
"""Summary

Long description...
"""
```

This could be done in apispec and it is compatible with the YAML docstring parsing:

```py
def my_view(...):
"""Summary

Long description...
---
YAML stuff
"""
```

I didn't take much time to look into it any deeper.

Marking as 1.0 as I would like to at least take needed utils out of yaml_utils.py (`trim_docstring`, maybe `dedent`) back into utils (those have no yaml dependency).

Marking as backward incompatible in case the feature is activated by default.

issue_title: Field validator as method is buggy/inconsistent
issue_body:
Let's take the example from the docs at http://marshmallow.readthedocs.org/en/latest/quickstart.html#field-validators-as-methods

If you run this:
```python
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, validates, ValidationError

class ItemSchema(Schema):
    quantity = fields.Integer()

    @validates('quantity')
    def validate_quantity(self, value):
        if value < 0:
            raise ValidationError('Quantity must be greater than 0.')
        if value > 30:
            raise ValidationError('Quantity must not be greater than 30.')

schema = ItemSchema()
result, errors = schema.load({'quantity': 40})
print("result:", result) 
print("errors:", errors)
```
you get the result:
```
result: {'quantity': 40}
errors: {'quantity': ['Quantity must not be greater than 30.']}
```
instead of the empty result `{}` (because validation failed).


Just for comparison, the behavior is correct for validation via lambda-function:
```python
class ItemSchema2(Schema):
    quantity = fields.Integer(validate=lambda x: 0 <= x <= 30)

schema = ItemSchema2()
result, errors = schema.load({'quantity': 40})
print("result:", result)
print("errors:", errors)
```
This time you get the expected result:
```
result: {}
errors: {'quantity': ['Invalid value.']}
```

issue_title: Deserializing only specific fields from a schema in fields.Nested?
issue_body:
The `only` parameter for [`fields.Nested`](http://marshmallow.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api_reference.html#marshmallow.fields.Nested) only applies to serialization.

Since you can only pass the schema class or name string to `fields.Nested`, there is currently no way to limit which fields are deserialized. `Schema.__init__()` allows use of `dump_only` and `load_only`, but these cannot currently be used with `fields.Nested`.

Is there a way to limit which fields are deserialized from a nested schema?

issue_title: Nesting: Referencing class object via database column
issue_body:
I'm building an application based on SQLAlchemy with plugin support.
Referencing relations and therewith creating Nested serializations between SQLAlchemy classes works perfectly as well as relating classes with each other via member variables.
However that application implements a plugin-system where I define a relation between a database row and a class-member variable defined by a plugged-in class.

Probably some example code will be best:

```
template_plugins = {'73ea9cc2': <class 'ims.models.TemplatePlugin.BestTemplateEver', ...>

class User(Base): # sqlalchemy table
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key = True)
    [..]
    country_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('countries.id'))       # [1]
    country = relationship("Country")                              # [1]
    template_plugin_id = Column(String(8)) # foreign key to a class mem # [2]

	class Schema(Sch):
		class Meta:
			country = fields.Nested(Country.Schema)                # [1]
			template_plugin = fields.Nested(TemplatePlugin.Schema)      # [2]


class Country(Base): #sqlalchemy table                             # [1]
    __tablename__ = 'countries'                                    # [1]
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key = True)                       # [1]
    code = Column(String(2), unique = True)                        # [1]
    name = Column(Unicode(64))                                     # [1]

    class Schema(Sch):                                             # [1]
        class Meta:                                                # [1]
            fields = ("id", "code", "name")                        # [1]


class TemplatePlugin(): # plugged-in class plugins will derive from     # [2]
    __metaclass__ = PluginMount                                         # [2]

    plugin_id = "73ea9cc2"                                              # [2]
    name = "Best template ever!"                                        # [2]

    def __init__(self).                                                 # [2]
		pass                                                            # [2]

    class Schema(Sch):                                                  # [2]
        class Meta:                                                     # [2]
            fields = ("id", "name") # [2]

                                                                   # [1] referencing serialized user_obj.country.name works as expected
                                                                        # [2] referencing serialized user_obj.template_plugin.name would be great to work, too...
```

So, is there any way I can reference a certain instance of TemplatePlugin() via User.Schema().template_plugin ?

Thanks a lot and keep up the good work!

issue_title: Senza traffic should wait for stack to be ready to change traffic
issue_body:
### Current Behaviour

When running change traffic `senza traffic` on stack recently deployed. The status code of the stack is not ready (`(CRETE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE`) and Senza fails trying to update it.
### Cause of the Problem

It is not possible to update CF stack when it's status code is not in `(CRETE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE` (or we should not change when it is not in one of those status code).
### Expected Behaviour

Senza should wait the stack to get in a final status code if the current status of the stack is `(CREATE|UPDATE)_IN_PROGRESS` and ending up in some good state `(CREATE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE`.

issue_title: Bug: unittest test_standard_iterator_unbuffered seems buggy
issue_body:
Subtle bug, but here it goes:

On test_standard_iterator_unbuffered (tests/unit/form_tests/utils_test/test_core_DataChunkIterator), line 57 and 59, we have
dci = DataChunkIterator(data=range(10), buffer_size=1)
self.assertTupleEqual(dci.max_shape, (None,))

I claim it should read:
self.assertTupleEqual(dci.max_shape, (10,))

This is because in the constructor of DataChunkIterator we have:

```        if self.max_shape is None:
            # If the self.data object identifies it shape then use it
            if hasattr(self.data,  "shape"):
                self.max_shape = self.data.shape
                # Avoid the special case of scalar values by making them into a 1D numpy array
                if len(self.max_shape) == 0:
                    self.data = np.asarray([self.data, ])
                    self.max_shape = self.data.shape
                    self.__data_iter = iter(self.data)
            # Try to get an accurate idea of max_shape for other Python datastructures if possible.
            # Don't just callget_shape for a generator as that would potentially trigger loading of all the data
            elif isinstance(self.data, list) or isinstance(self.data, tuple):
                self.max_shape = ShapeValidator.get_data_shape(self.data)```
```

Initially, max_shape is None will be true, and data will not have attribut.  So the flow control will pass to line ```elif isinstance(self.data, list) or isinstance(self.data, tuple):```

However, this will not evaluate to true in python3, because data is type "range" (in python 2, it is type list, so the line will evaluate True, and max_shape would be set to 10).  

In python3 max_shape will not be set, so the test will pass, but I think this is incorrect behavior for the test.  I think ```elif isinstance(self.data, list) or isinstance(self.data, tuple):``` should be replaced by ```elif isinstance(self.data, (list, tuple, range)):``` and tests or other code should be adjusted accordingly.  This is a short job, but one prone to mistakes, and since it involves rewriting test behavior, I vote for @ajtritt to make the modifications if he agrees.

issue_title: 为什么会有next_bar, 却没有current_bar_open?
issue_body:
在现实中的量化交易逻辑通常如下:
1. 利用历史数据来选股, 如果是以日为单位的话, 就是以昨天以前的数据来进行选股.

2. 在当前的bar中以当前的价格来成交,   在当前bar中可以知道的价格有开盘价和收盘价， 例如，如果是以日("1d")以上周期为单位的话,  开盘价成交表示是在早晨开盘时运行策略并以当时的市场价格成交，如果是以收盘价成交表示是在下午收盘前运行策略成交。

在运行策略的时候, 是不可能知道下一个bar的价格的, 甚至连下一个bar都不一定存在.

但是rqalpha却存在matching_type为"next_bar"的情况, 当然在回测中大部分情况下都可以获取未来的数据, 但是如果是运行昨天的回测呢, 它是没有next bar数据的。
如果把rqalpha接到实际的交易中, next_bar的运行逻辑是怎样的?

而且rqalpha当matching_type为"current_bar"时以收盘价成交而不是以开盘价成交也与其它回测框架不太一致。

issue_title: Un-friendly errors when there is a mis-configuration
issue_body:
I tried to run a LHCbTask (script below) and I got the following error:

```
ERROR    Exception occurred in task monitoring loop: <type 'exceptions.AttributeError'> 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'n_tosub'
The offending task was paused.
ERROR    Full traceback:
 Traceback (most recent call last):
   File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/6.7.2/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Tasks/TaskRegistry.py", line 92, in _thread_main
    p.update()
   File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/6.7.2/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Tasks/ITask.py", line 98, in update
    if trf.update() and not self.check_all_trfs:
   File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/6.7.2/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Tasks/ITransform.py", line 224, in update
    unit_status["bad"], self._getParent().n_tosub())
 AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'n_tosub' 
```

At a first glance I had literally no idea on what was going on. I think this kind of 

errors should raise a more friendly and understandable error message which can provide useful feedback for the users.

Thanks!
Carlos

```
'''
Generate a local MC production for a given event type

@author Miguel Ramos Pernas
'''

#
#    L o c a l    M C   p r o d u c t i o n           [sim09b  /  2016 MC  /  Stripping26]
#______________________________________________

# Run 200k - 100k per magnet.

runInAFS = False

EventType     = "43900075"
fileType      = 'DST'
evtsPerJob    = 1000
nJobs         = 1 # 100
magnet        = 'Down'
gaussVersion  = 'v49r8'
booleVersion  = 'v30r2'#p1'
mooreVersion  = 'v25r4'
brunelVersion = 'v50r2'
dvVersion     = 'v42r5p1'

decays = {'43900075' : 'mH125mA10tA10'}

decay = decays[EventType]

if magnet == 'Up':
    magnetconds = 'CondMagUp.py'
    polarity    = 'mu'
else:
    magnetconds = 'CondMagDown.py'
    polarity    = 'md'

nEvents      = evtsPerJob*nJobs
print '***', nEvents, 'events will be generated ***'

task         = LHCbTask()
task.name    = decay + "_Mag" + magnet
task.comment = "Local Production task"
task.float   = 10

import os
if runInAFS:
    software_path = '/afs/cern.ch/lhcb/software/releases/'
else:
    software_path = '/cvmfs/lhcb.cern.ch/lib/lhcb/'
APPCONFIGOPTS = software_path + "DBASE/AppConfig/v3r310/options"
#DECFILESROOT  = software_path + "DBASE/Gen/DecFiles/v29r14"
LBPYTHIA8ROOT = software_path + "GAUSS/GAUSS_"+gaussVersion+"/Gen/LbPythia8"
WORKSPACE     = os.getcwd()
DECFILESROOT = WORKSPACE

def genDiracBackend():
    '''
    Generate the Dirac backend, banning some sites
    '''
    backend = Dirac()
    backend.settings['BannedSites'] = ['LCG.RAL.uk', 'LCG.RAL-HEP.uk']
    return backend

availableSE = ['CBPF-USER', 'CERN-USER', 'CNAF-USER', 'CNAF-USER-2', 'CPPM-USER', 'GRIDKA-USER', 'IHEP-USER', 'IMPERIAL-USER', 'IN2P3-USER', 'IN2P3-USER-2', 'LAL-USER', 'Manchester-USER', 'NCBJ-USER', 'PIC-USER', 'SARA-USER']

#    G a u s s 

step1 = LHCbTransform(name="step1_Gauss",backend=genDiracBackend())
step1.application = Gauss(version = gaussVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc48-opt")
step1.application.optsfile = [	
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Gauss/Beam6500GeV-"+polarity+"100-2016-nu1.6.py"),
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Gauss/EnableSpillover-25ns.py"),
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Gauss/DataType-2016.py"),
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Gauss/RICHRandomHits.py"),
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Gauss/G4PL_FTFP_BERT_EmNoCuts.py"),
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Persistency/Compression-ZLIB-1.py"),

				File(DECFILESROOT  + "/options/" + EventType + ".py"),
				File(LBPYTHIA8ROOT + "/options/Pythia8.py"),

				File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)

                         ]

step1.application.extraopts = 'from Configurables import Gauss\n'\
    'from Gauss.Configuration import *\n'\
    'nEvts = ' + str(evtsPerJob) + '\n'\
    'LHCbApp().EvtMax = nEvts\n'

step1.mc_num_units = 1

step1.splitter = GaussSplitter(numberOfJobs=nJobs, eventsPerJob=evtsPerJob)
step1.outputfiles = [DiracFile("*.sim", locations = availableSE), LocalFile("*.root"), LocalFile("*.xml")]
step1.inputfiles = [LocalFile(WORKSPACE + "/decfiles/*.dec")]
task.appendTransform(step1)

#    B o o l e
 
step2 = LHCbTransform(name="step2_Boole",backend=genDiracBackend())
step2.application = Boole(version = booleVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt")
step2.application.optsfile = [	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Boole/Default.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Boole/EnableSpillover.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Boole/DataType-2015.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Boole/Boole-SetOdinRndTrigger.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Persistency/Compression-ZLIB-1.py"),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)
                         ]
datalink = TaskChainInput()
datalink.include_file_mask = ['\.sim$']
datalink.input_trf_id = step1.getID()
step2.addInputData(datalink)
step2.outputfiles = [DiracFile("*.digi", locations = availableSE), LocalFile("*.root"), LocalFile("*.xml")]
task.appendTransform(step2)

#    M o o r e    L 0

step3 = LHCbTransform(name="step3_Moore_L0",backend=genDiracBackend())
step3.application = Moore(version = mooreVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc48-opt")
step3.application.optsfile = [	
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/L0App/L0AppSimProduction.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/L0App/L0AppTCK-0x160F.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/L0App/ForceLUTVersionV8.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/L0App/DataType-2016.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Persistency/Compression-ZLIB-1.py"),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/CondL0.py"),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)
                         ]
datalink = TaskChainInput()
datalink.include_file_mask = ['\.digi$']
datalink.input_trf_id = step2.getID()
step3.addInputData(datalink)
step3.outputfiles = [DiracFile("*.digi", locations = availableSE)]
step3.delete_chain_input = True
task.appendTransform(step3)

#    M o o r e   H l t 1

step4 = LHCbTransform(name="step4_Moore_Hlt1",backend=genDiracBackend())
step4.application = Moore(version = mooreVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc48-opt")
step4.application.optsfile = [	
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Moore/MooreSimProductionForSeparateL0AppStep2015.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Conditions/TCK-0x5138160F.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Moore/MooreSimProductionHlt1.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Moore/DataType-2016.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Persistency/Compression-ZLIB-1.py"),
                                File(WORKSPACE + "/CondHlt1.py"),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)
                         ]
datalink = TaskChainInput()
datalink.include_file_mask = ['\.digi$']
datalink.input_trf_id = step3.getID()
step4.addInputData(datalink)
step4.outputfiles = [DiracFile("*.digi", locations = availableSE)]
step4.delete_chain_input = True
task.appendTransform(step4)

#    M o o r e   H l t 2

step5 = LHCbTransform(name="step5_Moore_Hlt2",backend=genDiracBackend())
step5.application = Moore(version = mooreVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc48-opt")
step5.application.optsfile = [	
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Moore/MooreSimProductionForSeparateL0AppStep2015.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Conditions/TCK-0x6139160F.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Moore/MooreSimProductionHlt2.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Moore/DataType-2016.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Persistency/Compression-ZLIB-1.py"),
                                File(WORKSPACE + "/CondHlt2.py"),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)
                         ]
datalink = TaskChainInput()
datalink.include_file_mask = ['\.digi$']
datalink.input_trf_id = step4.getID()
step5.addInputData(datalink)
step5.outputfiles = [DiracFile("*.digi", locations = availableSE), LocalFile("*.root"), LocalFile("*.xml")]
step5.delete_chain_input = True
task.appendTransform(step5)

#    B r u n e l 

step6 = LHCbTransform(name="step6_Brunel",backend=genDiracBackend())
step6.application = Brunel(version = brunelVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt")
step6.application.optsfile =  [	
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Brunel/MC-WithTruth.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Brunel/DataType-2016.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/Persistency/Compression-ZLIB-1.py"),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)
                         ]
datalink = TaskChainInput()
datalink.include_file_mask = ['\.digi$']
datalink.input_trf_id = step5.getID()
step6.addInputData(datalink)
step6.outputfiles = [DiracFile("*.dst", locations = availableSE), DiracFile("*.ldst", locations = availableSE), LocalFile("*.root"), LocalFile("*.xml")]
step6.delete_chain_input = True
task.appendTransform(step6)

#    D a V i n c i 

step7 = LHCbTransform(name="step7_DaVinci",backend=genDiracBackend())
step7.application = DaVinci(version = dvVersion, platform = "x86_64-slc6-gcc48-opt")
step7.application.optsfile = [	
				File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/DaVinci/DV-Stripping26-Stripping-MC-NoPrescaling-%s.py" %fileType),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/DaVinci/DataType-2016.py"),
                         	File(APPCONFIGOPTS + "/DaVinci/InputType-%s.py" %fileType),
                         	File(WORKSPACE + "/" + magnetconds)
                         ]
datalink = TaskChainInput()
datalink.include_file_mask = ['\.dst$', '\.ldst$']
datalink.input_trf_id = step6.getID()
step7.addInputData(datalink)
step7.outputfiles = [LocalFile("*.dst"), DiracFile("*.ldst", locations = availableSE), LocalFile("*.root"), LocalFile("*.xml")]
step7.delete_chain_input = True
task.appendTransform(step7)

task.run()
```
