issue_title: Meraki.commons.search error when getting Match History for specific summoners
issue_body:
I've written a crawler that grabs a summoners match history, checks each of its participants and and then iterates through their matches repeating the same process but on some rare occasions for specific summoners, I'll get an error that consistently appears for the same summoner only when I try and pull their match history. I've managed to pull the issue summoners match data at the start if I start with them, but if I grab their information with my crawler, that's when the issues happen.  It's consistent with doing it with the exact same summoners every time, but it only happens on those problem ones. 

From what I understand, the problem lies in getting a specific match in the match history, since I believe I'm grabbing the match history for the summoner, and every method I've used to check to make sure that I've got information is telling me that there is. I'm not sure if there is anything else I should be checking for, or what the problem might be as to why I'm getting this issue. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10048178/37867538-70a86f66-2f70-11e8-9cdb-bfac47d91cd2.png)

It could be that I just don't understand the search error exception that I'm getting where the number changes with the index i look at, but any help would be appreciated. 

I haven't touched python much before, most of my background has been in c++ so there might be some functions in python that I just don't know about , but this problem is overall just really annoying.

issue_title: AttributeError on attempt to resolve coroutine with experimental executor
issue_body:
I'd like to play with new experimental executor included with `graphql-core==1.1`, but unfortunately it didn't work well due to error below:

```
An error occurred while resolving field Query.allTeams
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/playpauseandstop/Projects/project/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphql/execution/experimental/resolver.py", line 30, in on_complete_resolver
    return result.then(on_resolve).then(__func).catch(on_error)
AttributeError: 'coroutine' object has no attribute 'then'
```

As well one more error occurred,

```
sys:1: RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'core_resolve_teams' was never awaited
```

----

My setup:

```
aiohttp==2.0.7
asyncpg==0.10.1
graphql-core==1.1
```

----

The code behind test:

**views.py**

```py
from aiohttp import web
from graphql import graphql
from graphql.execution import executor
from graphql.execution.executors.asyncio import AsyncioExecutor

from .schemas import GRAPHQL_CORE_SCHEMA


executor.use_experimental_executor = True


async def graphql_core(request: web.Request) -> web.Response:
    query = await request.json()['query']
    result = await graphql(
        GRAPHQL_CORE_SCHEMA,
        query,
        context_value={'db': request.app['db']},
        executor=AsyncioExecutor(loop=request.app.loop),
        return_promise=True)

    status = 400 if bool(result.errors) else 200
    return web.json_response(result, status=status)
```

**schemas.py**

```py
import graphql

from .resolvers import core_resolve_attr, core_resolve_teams


GRAPHQL_CORE_TEAM = graphql.GraphQLObjectType('Team', lambda: {
    'slug': graphql.GraphQLField(
        graphql.GraphQLString,
        resolver=core_resolve_attr()),
    'name': graphql.GraphQLField(
        graphql.GraphQLString,
        resolver=core_resolve_attr()),
    'originalName': graphql.GraphQLField(
        graphql.GraphQLString,
        resolver=core_resolve_attr('original_name'),
    ),
})


GRAPHQL_CORE_SCHEMA = graphql.GraphQLSchema(
    query=graphql.GraphQLObjectType('Query', lambda: {
        'allTeams': graphql.GraphQLField(
            graphql.GraphQLList(GRAPHQL_CORE_TEAM),
            resolver=core_resolve_teams,
        ),
    }),
)
```

**resolvers.py**

```py
def core_resolve_attr(name: str=None) -> Callable:
    def resolver(source: Any, args: Any, context: Any, info: Any) -> Any:
        return source[name or info.field_name]
    return resolver


async def core_resolve_teams(source: Any,
                             args: Any,
                             context: Any,
                             info: Any) -> list:
    async with context['db'].acquire() as conn:
        return await conn.fetch('TABLE teams')
```

----

But when I commented (removed) `executor.use_experimental_executor = True` line everything works fine and,

```
{
    allTeams {
        slug
    }
}
```

GraphQL query executed well.

Any ideas on why the `AttributeError` error happened with experimental executor? Or this error should be reported to https://github.com/syrusakbary/promise instead?

issue_title: Add visual indication that parsing isn't complete for some logs
issue_body:
When launching tensorflow with a logdir that contains logs from several runs, it reports their presence in the side-bar but until it has completed parsing the logs, data is still not presented. Thanks for your work on this project. It makes using TensorFlow much more pleasant!

issue_title: Training on selected subset of Dask DataFrame rows
issue_body:
In order to do CV iterations, I'd like to load all the data into a Dask DataFrame, select a subset of rows, and then train only on those selected rows. I've written some code that does this and it works when I use a ```LocalCluster```. When I try and use a true distributed cluster, I get an error when constructing the worker_map:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/proth/.conda/envs/dask/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dask_xgboost/core.py", line 116, in _train
    worker_map[first(workers)].append(key_to_part_dict[key])
  File "/home/proth/.conda/envs/dask/lib/python3.5/site-packages/toolz/itertoolz.py", line 368, in first
    return next(iter(seq))
StopIteration
```

I tried to write a test that demonstrates this behavior, but the test passes. This makes me think that ```distributed.utils_test.gen_cluster``` uses a ```LocalCluster```. Is there anyway to run this test with a true distributed cluster?

```
@gen_cluster(client=True, timeout=None)
def test_subset(c, s, a, b):
    # Train in xgboost with selected data
    dtrain = xgb.DMatrix(df[df.x < 8], label=labels[:7])
    bst = xgb.train(param, dtrain)

    # Combine data and labels and select rows together
    newdf = df.copy()
    newdf["labels"] = labels
    ddf = dd.from_pandas(newdf, npartitions=4)
    ddf_trimmed = ddf[ddf.x < 8]

    # Split data and labels back apart
    ddf_train = ddf_trimmed.drop("labels", axis=1)
    ddf_labels = ddf_trimmed["labels"]

    # Train in dask-xgboost with trimmed data (fails with distributed cluster!)
    dbst = yield dxgb._train(c, param, ddf_train, ddf_labels)

    result = bst.predict(dtrain)
    dresult = dbst.predict(dtrain)

    correct = (result > 0.5) == labels[:7]
    dcorrect = (dresult > 0.5) == labels[:7]
    assert dcorrect.sum() == correct.sum()
```

issue_title: Consider adding optional persistent history
issue_body:
It would be nice if there was an easy way to add persistent history to ``cmd2`` applications.

We would need to decide if this is done for readline history, cmd **history** command history, or both.  Perhaps there would be two separate options for making each of these persistent?

We could probably have an optional argument or two (with default values) to the cmd2.Cmd.__init__() initializer.

[This](https://kushaldas.in/posts/developing-command-line-interpreters-using-python-cmd2.html) blog post demonstrates the basics of how to make the readline history persistent:

```Python
import os
import atexit
import readline

history_file = os.path.expanduser('~/.mycli_history')
if not os.path.exists(history_file):
    with open(history_file, "w") as fobj:
        fobj.write("")
readline.read_history_file(history_file)
atexit.register(readline.write_history_file, history_file)
```

For ``cmd2`` **history** command serialization, we could probably just serialize/deserialize the contents of self.history using the ``pickle`` module.

issue_title: Support Postmark delivery webhook event
issue_body:
Postmark has added support for delivery notification: https://postmarkapp.com/blog/feature-announcement-webhook-for-delivery-events

Anymail should include these events in its normalized tracking webhook.

issue_title: Addition to Upgrade Guide for v2.2
Prevent lossy conversions
issue with unique_ptr ?
issue_body:
Hi,

I came across the following issue today which seems like a bug.

I have a class `Foo` that produces `Bar` and returns a unique_ptr to an instance of `Bar`.

On running a C++11 executable it works fine (little main in the code attached).
On running python, it craps out with 
```
RuntimeError: return_value_policy = move, but the object is neither movable nor copyable!
```
Am I missing something ? The class `Bar` is moveable, and copyable...

Code attached to reproduce.


[pybind11_issue_unique_ptr.zip](https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/files/715271/pybind11_issue_unique_ptr.zip)

It would be better if the error was wrapped in a `UserError` which prefixes the original error message with something like `Error while reading token file: ` or something like that.

issue_title: Add option to log to one file only
issue_body:
Feature request!

I'd like to have the option to log to one file only in the log_dir (or be able to specifically set a singular log and not have it rotate), this helps with docker logs ala nginx e.g. in Dockerfile

RUN ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log

OR have the ability to print the errors that _would_ be in the log to stdout

issue_title: --renew-hook script never runs for certonly staging force-renewal
issue_body:
I had created a certificate using a command like this:

    certbot certonly --staging --agree-tos --webroot -w /var/www/example.com/ -d example.com

I created a test script for post renewal

    #!/bin/sh
    set -e 
    echo "RENEWED_LINEAGE: $RENEWED_LINEAGE"
    echo "RENEWED_DOMAINS: $RENEWED_DOMAINS"

And I run certbot to force a renewal:

    $ sudo certbot --staging renew --force-renewal --renew-hook /home/steveo/config/scripts/lets-encrypt-renew-hook.sh 
    -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/example.com.conf
    -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    
    -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    new certificate deployed without reload, fullchain is
    /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem
    -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    
    Congratulations, all renewals succeeded. The following certs have been renewed:
    /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem (success)

But my renew hook script never ran.   If it had, I would expect to see it print the environment variables.

issue_body: For instance, something like `--force-version 0.8.1`

issue_title: Minor errors in CPS
issue_body:
The CPS available at http://cps.letsencrypt.org/ has some errors:

3.2.2.2 For DV-SSL Certificates, the CA provides a secure means of validating the Applicant’s control over, the device and domain name for which a Certificate is requested. 
- There should be no comma between "control over" and "the device".

4.6.1 Circumstance for Certificate Renewal
A Certificate may be renewed if the Key Pair has not reached the end of its validity, the Private Key has not been compromised, and the Certificate information and attributes are correct. Thus, the CA may choose to implement a three-year re-key period with an initial issue and two annual renewals before Renewal is required. The old Certificate need not be revoked, but must not be further renewed or updated.

-Is this supposed to say -- as written -- "Thus, the CA may choose to implement a three-year re-key period with an initial issue and two annual renewals before Renewal is required." or should it say "...a three-year renewal period...before re-keying is required"? The way it's written in the CPS doesn't make much sense to me.

issue_title: Storage delete operations are not sync
issue_body:
As pointed out by @craigcitro, `Object.delete` and `Bucket.delete` actually submit delete jobs to the storage service, so they're not exactly synchronous operations, which is different from user expectations.

We should explore either waiting on the jobs to finish as part of the API itself, or rename these operations to add a `_async` suffix.

An example such failure is here: https://travis-ci.org/googledatalab/pydatalab/jobs/327385568

issue_title: Remove expired locks
issue_body:
A raiden payment channel is comprised of two ends, one for each participant, each participant is responsable to control the state changes for it's own end, this is done to ensure the messages sent are valid.

- [ ] Add a updating message from the sender to the partner.
- [ ] Add a watcher to remove expired blocks based on `tasks.py:AlarmTask`
  - [ ] Clear the node's internal state for the expired lock and send the message to the partner

issue_title: width/height attributes often not removed with --enable-viewboxing due to rounding errors
issue_body:
The function [`properlySizeDoc`](https://github.com/scour-project/scour/blob/210c5f64abdf336e25c58ca927093b3aa94c0a0c/scour/scour.py#L3024) is supposed to set a `viewBox` with the images dimensions and remove `width`/`height` attributes.

The problem is that we preserve `width`/`height` if a `viewBox` is already present and the values of both do not match perfectly.

Due to rounding in Scour they will almost never match for non-integer dimensions.

issue_title: Implement a standard date partitioning strategy for indexed data relying on index names
issue_body:
Relying on #138 , implement a date partitioning system such as index-root-name-YYYY-MM :
- `index-root-name` will be defined in collection reference (indexPatternPath) and cannot contains wildcards or multi-indeces definitions (such as coma separated indeces)
- `YYYY-MM` will be defined as default date pattern
- a custom date pattern could be set in collection's custom params and must be consistant with ISO 8601 date patterns (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)
- date partitioning system should be an optional feature when creating a collection reference and collections that use this strategy must be flagged easily (simply by checking their custom params perhaps?)

As a result, for those collections, Explore API response for time filtered requests have to restrict their ES requests to relevant indeces (that is to say use the proper indeces pattern for the ES request, for example `index-root-name-2017-12` or `index-root-name-2017-*`)

issue_title: Consider deprecating travis:after in favor of Build Stages
issue_body:
https://blog.travis-ci.com/2017-05-11-introducing-build-stages

I don't have any plans to remove travis:after, but with the announcement of build stages for Travis, I'm watching it closely, and considering whether it will fully cover the use-cases that travis:after does. I suspect that it will end up as the better option, and that we will end up adding a note to the docs declaring that the better fix.

Right now it's still early days, and we need to watch and see. There are still bugs in this beta feature that need to be ironed out, but I'm very glad to see Travis supporting this feature natively.

issue_title: Unable to run fast.ai on self compiled pytorch under ubuntu :(
issue_body:
Hello everyone,

thanks not only for developing and sharing fast.ai as a library, but also for the wonderful courses and the community around this whole topic!

Unfortunately I am currently not able to use the library, due to the fact of a non functioning precompiled pytorch (supplied by your install script for your paperspace solution).
I am using an 'old' AMD cpu, which does not offer the required instruction set demanded by the precompiled pytorch package. Thus I am forced to compile it on my own...

I am running an ubuntu node locally. I do not mind the release version. I am free to swap as I like. Currently I am running 17.10, but also tested 16.04. I am willing to switch to any distribution (headless release), as long as I can manage to run your code.

I installed http://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/repos/ubuntu1604/x86_64/cuda-repo-ubuntu1604_9.0.176-1_amd64.deb and http://files.fast.ai/files/cudnn-9.1-linux-x64-v7.tgz (see http://files.fast.ai/setup/paperspace).

My python (3.6.3) packages are the following:

widgetsnbextension 3.1.4

I cloned pytorch v0.3.1 from https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch and compiled with an ancient gcc version (see https://medium.com/@Inoryy/compiling-pytorch-4-0-on-ubuntu-17-10-with-cuda-9-0-and-python-3-6-6769a0df56d5).

When I try to run
`from fastai.imports import *`
`from fastai.transforms import *`
`from fastai.conv_learner import *`
`from fastai.model import *`
`from fastai.dataset import *`
`from fastai.sgdr import *`
`from fastai.plots import *`
`PATH = "data/dogscats/"`
`sz=224`
`torch.cuda.is_available()`
`torch.backends.cudnn.enabled`
`arch=resnet34`
`data = ImageClassifierData.from_paths(PATH, tfms=tfms_from_model(arch, sz))`
`learn = ConvLearner.pretrained(arch, data, precompute=True)`
the last line produces a crash.

Line 20 `res = torch.FloatTensor(a.astype(np.float32))` in `fastai/courses/dl1/fastai/core.py` generates the runtime error `RuntimeError: tried to construct a tensor from a nested float sequence, but found an item of type numpy.float32 at index (0, 0, 0, 0)`.
If I change this line to `res = torch.FloatTensor(a.astype(np.float32).tolist())` and also adjust line 18, the error will disappear and the code runs for a very long time (break after approx. 20mins). There is no progress made during this time. The code is clearing hanging somewhere... Please help me to debug this further?!

Yes cuda is up and running. Both `torch.cuda.is_available()` and `torch.backends.cudnn.enabled` return `True`.

Which pytorch version is the minimal required one? Please help me to debug this issue in order to resolve it.

issue_body:
Hi,

I have been using the pretrained ResNet model which works fine, however when I switched to the pretrained VGG-19 at the master branch, I found that the output of the the pytorch verison is very different from the caffe version.  The first predicted class for this [https://github.com/BVLC/caffe/blob/master/examples/images/cat.jpg](url) is 644 which is wrong. 
The preprocessing that I used is what was suggested by @soumith   here [https://discuss.pytorch.org/t/whats-the-range-of-the-input-value-desired-to-use-pretrained-resnet152-and-vgg19/1683](url) 


`import torchvision.models as models`
`from torchvision import transforms as trn`
`import skimage.io`
`import skimage`

`preprocess = trn.Compose([
    trn.Normalize([0.485, 0.456, 0.406], [0.229, 0.224, 0.225])
])`

`vgg19 = models.vgg19()`
`vgg19.load_state_dict(torch.load(os.path.join(model_path, 'vgg19-dcbb9e9d.pth')))`
`vgg19.cuda()`
`vgg19.eval()`

`I = skimage.io.imread(image_path)`

`I = crop_image(I)`
`I = torch.from_numpy(I.transpose([2, 0, 1])).cuda()`
`I = Variable(preprocess(I), volatile=True)`
`output = vgg19.forward(I.unsqueeze(0))`
`output = F.softmax(output, dim=1)`

Should the preprocessing be different from the one suggested by @soumith ?
Thanks,

issue_title: A base class for all transforms
Python resources must set outputs received from engine
issue_body:
Currently it does not:

https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/blob/c2b2f3b117963bbddf869c483a4ee1e8d45475f7/sdk/python/lib/pulumi/resource.py#L76-L79

`tfgen` generated providers accomplish this by manually setting properties in the custom resource's constructor, which probably not what it should be doing (the `Resource` constructor should be doing this).

issue_title: Attach shell (in cluster setting) without passing in addresses
issue_body:
Once we've started a ray cluster using `cluster.py`, we should be able to ssh to the head node and attach a shell to the cluster with `python scripts/shell.py --attach` without having to pass in the addresses of the scheduler, object store, and shell.

One way to do this is to have `cluster.py` save the addresses in a file somewhere on he head node and let `shell.py` read from it.

issue_title: [tune] Race condition for short running trainable functions
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

```
        result = self._status_reporter._get_and_clear_status()
        while result is None or \
                time.time() - poll_start < \
                self.config["script_min_iter_time_s"]:
            time.sleep(1)
            result = self._status_reporter._get_and_clear_status()
```

In this loop, statuses may be lost permanently. This is problematic for functions that report only a single status and return immediately.

cc @vlad17 @richardliaw

issue_title: Wrong image saving
issue_body:
Strange condition at the `104` line in `image.py` (https://github.com/pdfminer/pdfminer.six/blob/master/pdfminer/image.py#L104).

`image.colorspace` value:
```
result = {list} <class 'list'>: [/'DeviceRGB']
 0 = {PSLiteral} /'DeviceRGB'
 __len__ = {int} 1
```
But condition checks list with string value and always equals false and instead of saving picture to BMP it save as raw data.

Is this condition wrong? Or this is feature?

![proof](http://s1.micp.ru/a8B19.png)

issue_title: Switch all subclasses to use super
Manager.from_connection is broken
issue_body:
Since when calling `Manager.from_connection`, it defers to `BaseManager.from_connection`. Inside this implementation, it uses `cls.__call__` to instantiate. This would be fine, but `Manager.__init__` function redefines the arguments, and it gets confused. In the end, it passes an Engine into `sqlalchemy.create_engine`. 

Thanks @ddomingof and @bgyori for pointing this out

References sorgerlab/indra#575

Fix interactive plot flicker via own layout
issue_body:
Been hitting a bugaboo (esp. in sage) where cells don't stay one height when their plot vanishes for recomputation. So everything below in the notebook bounces around.

But the interactive is a layout, so we can use CSS to fix height at an appropriate value (currently chosen manually) for a given matplotlib call.
```
interactive_plot = interactive(f, m=(-2.0, 2.0), b=(-3, 3, 0.5))
interactive_plot.layout.height = '350px'
interactive_plot
```
in the context of
```
%matplotlib inline
from ipywidgets import interactive
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

def f(m, b):
    plt.figure(2)
    x = np.linspace(-10, 10, num=1000)
    plt.plot(x, m * x + b)
    plt.ylim(-5, 5)
    plt.show()
```

issue_title: Create a Log output widget to log any output from sync messages
issue_body:
In #1320, we have the problem that a sync message is causing an error that has no place to be displayed in the front end. Until the front end has a place to display unhandled output, perhaps we should create an output widget that stores any output from sync handling.

I *think* this should be fairly straightforward - create an output widget when widgets are imported, and make sure we are using the output widget when we actually set the state of any widget.

issue_title: Very slow to load data when resampling requested in query
issue_body:
### Expected behaviour
Resampling of Landsat to 10m x 10m pixels for comparison with Sentinel 2 would not have a great impact on the speed of `dc.load`.

### Actual behaviour
`dc.load` slowed to the point where it was essentially unusable. e.g. Began extraction prior to a 1 hour meeting, and it hadn't finished loading in the tiny area (all time, all bands) by the time I came back.

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour
`ds = dc.load(product = ls5_nbar_albers, group_by = 'solar_day', **query)`

```
query = {'output_crs': 'EPSG:3577',
         'resolution': (-10, 10),
         'geopolygon': geom
        }
```
Note that the geopolygon is not the problem - same issue encountered when a small bounding box was used instead. Area for extraction is very small (~50 by ~70 pixels in Sentinel2)

### Environment information

* Which ``datacube --version`` are you using?
* What datacube deployment/enviornment are you running against?
DEA on the VDI, loaded with 
```
module use /g/data/v10/public/modules/modulefiles/
module load agdc-py3-prod
```

issue_title: Fix environment variable encodings in `vistir.misc.run`
easy_install script fails creating virtualenv in path deeper than 78 chars
issue_body:
This is a regression after 1.6. In 1.6 this was not a problem, so I can only assume it didn't execute <venv>/bin/easy_install directly. The problem occurs during the creation of the virtual environment, when it tries to install pip it seems to be executing the easy_install script directly which is a problem when it contains a shebang line that's longer than 80 characters.

A simple work around is to execute the virtual environment's python, passing it <venv>/bin/easy_install as an argument. I've had to use this work around on a number of occasions and haven't had any issues.

This is a fairly serious problem for me.

issue_title: @type value should be an IRI
issue_body:
According to section 3.3 of JSON-LD (http://json-ld.org/spec/latest/json-ld-syntax/#specifying-the-type) , the value of the "@type" property should always be an IRI. So the following code

```python
  pyld.jsonld.triples({"@type": "http://example.com/Thing"})
```

should generate

```python
  [{'o': {'@id': 'http://example.com/Thing'}, 'p': '@type', 's': '_:c14n0'}]
```

but it generates

```python
  [{'o': 'http://example.com/Thing', 'p': '@type', 's': '_:c14n0'}]
```

issue_title: subscription.attributes lists nonexistant plan_code
issue_body:
the .attributes property for subscription objects includes attributes that don't actually exist on the object.  I've seen this in other objects, too, so I don't think it's specific to that model.

For example, 'plan_code' is listed in the subscription model's "attributes" list, but a subscription object has no 'plan_code' attribute.

Therefore, the following fails:

    account = recurly.Account.get('some_email@example.com')
    subscriptions = account.subscriptions()
    sub = subscriptions[0]
    'plan_code' in sub.attributes # returns true
    sub.plan_code # throws attribute error

However, the plan_code *is* available for this object by the following mechanism:

`sub.plan.plan_code`

A break in the attributes list means that you can't really do anything dynamic with the attributes list, like create a dictionary with the object:

`dict((attrib, getattr(sub, attrib)) for attrib in sub.attributes)`

As a result, it's impossible to create a general mechanism for transforming a Recurly object.  With the dictionary example, it means that it's impossible to create a mechanism to render arbitrary Recurly objects in a web page; you would have to write a custom render function for every class exposed by the API if you wanted to do that.

The attributes list appears to be used erroneously by the client library itself.  For example, the .to_element method generates an xml.etree.ElementTree.Element object, but fails silently at generating elements for the attributes in the attribute list that do not really exist.  As a result, if you use .to_element on a subscription object, the Element instance that is returned is not representative of what the API returns.  WIth the example of subscriptions, the resulting .to_element Element contains no reference back to the Subscription Plan that the user has subscribed to:

    In [10]: e = sub.to_element()

    In [11]: list(e)
    Out[11]: 
    [<Element 'uuid' at 0x102f48e90>,
     <Element 'state' at 0x102f48ed0>,
     <Element 'quantity' at 0x102f48f10>,
     <Element 'activated_at' at 0x102f48f50>,
     <Element 'canceled_at' at 0x102f485d0>,
     <Element 'expires_at' at 0x102f48650>,
     <Element 'current_period_started_at' at 0x102f48cd0>,
     <Element 'current_period_ends_at' at 0x102f48d90>,
     <Element 'trial_started_at' at 0x102f3abd0>,
     <Element 'trial_ends_at' at 0x102f3ab50>,
     <Element 'unit_amount_in_cents' at 0x102f3a610>,
     <Element 'total_billing_cycles' at 0x102f3aad0>,
     <Element 'currency' at 0x102f4a0d0>,
     <Element 'subscription_add_ons' at 0x102f4a150>,
     <Element 'account' at 0x102f4a1d0>]
